key_entities.name: Orders
key_entities.description: Central transaction records capturing customer purchase events.

key_entities.name: Order Items
key_entities.description: Detailed line items for orders, linking products and supplies to specific transactions.

key_entities.name: Customers
key_entities.description: Core entity representing individuals who interact with the store and place orders.

key_entities.name: Locations
key_entities.description: Physical stores or distribution points where orders are fulfilled.

key_entities.name: Products
key_entities.description: The catalog of items available for sale in the e-commerce platform.

relationships: Staging models (stg_*) act as a direct cleaning layer for Raw source tables.
The 'order_items' mart aggregates data from four distinct staging sources: items, orders, products, and supplies.
Marts models like 'customers' and 'orders' serve as the final analytical layer for downstream reporting.
A MetricFlow time spine is implemented to support time-series analysis within a semantic layer.

risks.critical_paths: The 'order_items' transformation (models\marts\order_items.sql) is a high-dependency node, consuming four upstream staging tables. Any failure in stg_order_items, stg_orders, stg_products, or stg_supplies will break the core sales reporting.
risks.recommendations: The script '.github\workflows\scripts\dbt_cloud_run_job.py' is flagged as a dead code candidate with a high complexity score (6.0); it should be audited and either integrated into the CI/CD pipeline or removed to reduce technical debt.

architecture: This is a dbt (Data Build Tool) project following a Medallion-style architecture consisting of a Staging layer (source alignment and cleaning) and a Marts layer (business logic and dimensional modeling). It utilizes SQL-based transformations and includes a semantic layer component for time-based metrics.
